questions.question: How many main rounds did the NIST PQC evaluation process have before the initial selections?
questions.explanation: NIST conducted 3 main evaluation rounds: Round 1 (2017-2019), Round 2 (2019-2020), and Round 3 (2020-2022), before announcing the initial selections in July 2022.

questions.question: What is NIST Security Category 5 equivalent to in terms of attack difficulty?
questions.explanation: Security Category 5 is defined as being at least as hard as AES-256 key recovery, representing the highest security level in the NIST PQC framework.

questions.question: Which multivariate signature scheme was broken during Round 3?
questions.explanation: Rainbow, a multivariate signature scheme and Round 3 finalist, was broken by Ward Beullens in 2022 who found an attack that could recover secret keys in practical time.

questions.question: Why did NIST select SLH-DSA (SPHINCS+) despite its larger signature sizes?

options.text: It was the fastest signature scheme
It provides mathematical diversity with hash-only security
It had the smallest public keys
It was the only signature scheme submitted

questions.explanation: SLH-DSA was selected because its security relies only on hash function properties, providing mathematical diversity and a conservative backup in case lattice-based schemes face unexpected attacks.

questions.question: What document did NIST publish to explain their selection rationale?
questions.explanation: NIST IR 8413, titled 'Status Report on the Third Round of the NIST PQC Process', explained the rationale for selecting finalists and the trade-offs considered.

questions.question: How many algorithms advanced from Round 1 to Round 2?
questions.explanation: 26 algorithms advanced from Round 1 to Round 2, down from 69 candidates that were initially accepted for Round 1 evaluation.

questions.question: NIST's primary evaluation criterion was algorithm performance (speed and size).
questions.explanation: Security was NIST's primary evaluation criterion. NIST explicitly stated that 'security will be weighted heavily.' Performance was important but secondary to security considerations.
